name: Central tolerance defects
one_liner: Bad thymic education lets autoreactive lymphocytes escape deletion.

name: Peripheral tolerance / Treg and checkpoint failure
one_liner: Regulatory circuits that should restrain activated lymphocytes fail outside the thymus.

name: Apoptosis / contraction failure
one_liner: Activated lymphocytes that should die after an immune response remain alive and accumulate.

name: Inflammatory brake failure
one_liner: Pathways that normally damp inflammatory signaling fail, producing chronic innate immune activation.

name: Interferonopathies
one_liner: Self nucleic acids or aberrant sensing pathways drive persistent type I interferon signaling.

name: JAK–STAT pathway GOF
one_liner: Cytokine signaling is locked too far on, reshaping immune differentiation and tolerance.

name: Complement / clearance defects
one_liner: Failure to clear immune complexes or extracellular chromatin turns self debris into antigen.

name: B-cell tolerance / CVID-like autoimmunity overlap
one_liner: B-cell deletion, maturation, or signaling is distorted, producing autoantibodies alongside immunodeficiency.
